title: Where Mirrors Become Earth

features.name: the last reflection
features.desc-on-touch: a pool that mirrors earth instead of sky; the water has learned to reflect only what it has become

features.name: the mirror shard becoming root
features.desc-on-touch: a piece of broken glass with vine curling around it; the mirror and the green have accepted each other without speaking

features.name: the earthing point
features.desc-on-touch: a node where the diagnostic signal has given up trying to reflect; it simply touches the earth and listens

features.name: the boundary where both things know each other
features.desc-on-touch: the frame where mirror-logic and earth-logic overlap; neither can entirely become the other, but they share an edge

features.name: the corrupted glass-memory
features.desc-on-touch: layers of glass and soil compressed together; the corruption has made them inseparable, and both have stopped trying to unmix

inscription: the mirror shows you what it has become; the older it is, the more earth lives inside

file.name: Mirror_Study_Coastal_Fragment.txt
file.note: reflection documentation corrupted with fragments of a broadcast—mentions of incision and ceremony, a beach at dusk where an operation became landscape

exits.label: up to the vault where glass still remembers it was clear
deeper to where the soil has forgotten what it reflects
toward the deep where mirrors stop knowing themselves
